commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a CONTRIBUTING.md section asking issue reporters to state whether they plan to submit a fix, want maintainer guidance, can help test, or are only reporting for someone else to pick up.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is contributor documentation and process guidance rather than a reported runtime bug. The relevant check is whether current main already contains or duplicates the requested wording, and it does not.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer acceptance of the contributor-process wording plus contributor proof or a proof override; there is no narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: Add issue contribution intent guidance
target.body:
﻿## Summary

Adds a short "When Opening Issues" section to `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

The new guidance asks issue reporters to mention, when they already know, whether they plan to work on a PR themselves, want maintainer guidance first, can help test, or are only reporting the issue for someone else to pick up.

## Why

This is intended as a soft coordination signal, not an assignment or commitment system. It should help avoid duplicate work when someone opens an issue as a tracking note before creating their own PR, while still making clear that maintainers may retitle, close, reprioritize, or accept another PR if needed.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CONTRIBUTING.md`
- `git diff --check`
